title: ncarnate verified-netCDF4 handoff record (OperationResult, schema v2)
description: One executed conversion, fully described: the frozen handoff a downstream (e.g. a Zarr tail) validates a received record against and builds an array spec from without re-opening the netCDF or reading ncarnate internals (gate G5). Exactly one OperationResult.to_record() per record. The `verification` record attests conversion INTEGRITY — re-read value identity within the dtype scope, at the per-verifier fidelity scope its `method` field states (see docs/fidelity-notes.md) — NOT scientific correctness, which is the separate `validation` record ncarnate never performs.

operation.description: The executed operation token: `recompress` (netCDF/HDF5 in place) or `convert` (HDF4/HDF-EOS2 to a new netCDF4).

plan_hash.description: sha256 hex of the canonical executed-plan projection (operation + requested options + source identity: detected_format, size_bytes, sha256). A stable, ncarnate/library-version-independent identity of the request, for idempotent/retry-safe materialization. COLLISION CAVEAT: distinct only when source.sha256 is non-null; under --allow-unverified the digest is null and the key degrades to {operation, options, format, size} — a consumer must not key idempotency on a null-digest plan.

retention.description: Caller/pipeline-owned retention metadata. ncarnate NEVER sets this: it always emits null, reserving the slot. Interior is intentionally open (not ours to constrain).

sha256.description: sha256 hex of the source bytes ncarnate read, or null when hashing was relaxed (--allow-unverified).
sha256 hex of the committed output. HDF5-library-version-dependent, so excluded from the canonical hash.

encodingOptions.description: The REQUESTED run encoding. Per-variable EFFECTIVE encoding is on each variable, read back from the output.

groupNode.description: One group of the output, recursively (root path `/`). Empty/metadata-only groups are included so the HDFEOS_INFORMATION group carrying verbatim StructMetadata.0 survives into the handoff.

dimension.description: A group dimension: name, size, and whether it is an appendable unlimited/record dimension. Gives a consumer the array shape a variable's `dimensions` names resolve against.

dtype.description: numpy dtype string (var.dtype.str, e.g. `<i2`, `|i1`). Carries byteorder; a consumer reconstructs the Zarr dtype from THIS, never from `endian`.

endian.description: `native` | `big` | `little`. `native` is not a concrete order — read byteorder from `dtype`.

dimensions.description: The dimension NAMES this variable spans; shape resolves against the group's `dimensions` sizes.

encoding.description: The EFFECTIVE per-variable encoding read back from the committed output.

chunksizes.description: Per-dimension chunk lengths, or null for contiguous storage (a consumer falls back to the full shape).

attributes.description: Full attribute set including the scale_factor / add_offset / _FillValue packing declarations the fidelity contract preserves. _FillValue may be ABSENT (the source declared none).

storage_type.description: `NC_STRING` | `NC_CHAR` for text (the KD-L6 distinction), or the numpy dtype string for a numeric attribute.

value.description: A JSON-safe attribute value: a scalar, an array, or a non-finite-float string token (`NaN`/`Infinity`/`-Infinity`). Never a bare object.

nameMapping.description: A sanitized-name companion: the output name, the original HDF4/HDF-EOS2 name, its kind, and the parent_path of its owner (which disambiguates an attribute rename that recurs on many owners).

generated.description: Reconstructed coordinate / grid-mapping variable paths (empty on the storage-only recompression path).

code.description: A stable ncarnate.audit.codes registry code where one applies, else null.

verificationRecord.description: Conversion-INTEGRITY status — ncarnate always fills this. A failed verification raises and leaves no output, so `status` has no failure value. `method` states the exact fidelity scope PER VERIFIER: re-read value identity within the dtype scope on both paths; the netCDF path is storage-only, while the HDF4 path additionally reconstructs geolocation ADDITIVELY (the output tree is a superset of the SDS payload) and transforms character-attribute NUL padding (docs/fidelity-notes.md). It never attests scientific correctness.

validationRecord.description: Scientific-VALIDATION status — structurally separate from conversion integrity. ncarnate never performs it and only ever sets status=`not_performed`, leaving validator/method/record for a downstream pipeline to fill.

record.description: The pipeline's validation evidence. Interior intentionally open.

adapter_versions.description: Native-library versions that produced the output, keyed by adapter name (numpy, netCDF4, netcdf_c, libhdf5, pyhdf, libhdf4). The key set is a probe list (additive), so it is open-valued; an adapter absent from the install maps to null.

resultWarning.description: A non-fatal note about a successful conversion, carrying a stable ncarnate.audit.codes registry code.

context.description: Per-warning context; interior open (matches the audit issue context).
